title: Resident Evil 4
subtitle: Remake (the video game)
review: I think they've achieved perfection here, by eschewing the maze and puzzle style of the first couple of games, and by embracing action more confidently than the remake of {Resident Evil 3}. It might not be everyone's favourite style of Resi game, but it's undoubtedly thrilling, and the combat is perfectly tuned for large groups of enemies. Leon is powerful and agile, and is fed a constant stream of ammunition to encourage you to stay on the offensive. In {Resident Evil 2} zombies don't drop items when they die, so shooting them strictly consumes ammo, and it's sometimes better to run past them. Here, you still have to manage the contents your attaché case, but clearing every enemy is worth it because of the loot they drop. At times it feels like you're playing as Indiana Jones, and yet you'll panic and run when ambushed by new threats in a way that no movie action hero would. There's a surprising amount of variation in the gameplay, for a survival horror game, ranging from cheesy action tropes to brilliant Ashley mission in the Library. There are so many weapons that you'd never use them all in a single playthrough, providing an inherent reward for replaying the game, without the need for recycling the content into some kind of Claire Redfield mode. I loved it, and I was very impressed with how polished, beautiful, stylish and well-optimised it was. The music is also great, especially during boss battles.
